doc: Declarative backend manifest — the single source of truth for HOW each backend is called (Constitution Law 6). Each platform implements ONE generic interpreter that reads this. Today the Windows adapters hardcode the same facts; refactoring them to read this file is a tracked PARITY task. Placeholders {model}/{text}/{apiKey}/... come from config.json. Verified live: claude + codex (CLI) translate end-to-end.

claude.notes: prompt via stdin avoids cmd.exe/shell arg mangling; spawn from a neutral CWD so it doesn't load the current project's context. --effort is appended only when a reasoning tier is set; the model alias is the primary capability tier.

codex.notes: trailing '-' reads stdin (avoids the non-TTY hang); chrome goes to stderr. effort via -c model_reasoning_effort (inline, always-on, default low). Doctor matches the `login status` signature; do NOT gate on `codex doctor` top-level overallStatus (npm-root noise).

copilot.notes: Non-interactive form verified vs `copilot --help`: `-p <prompt> --model <m> --allow-all-tools` (--allow-all-tools is REQUIRED for non-interactive; old `-s`/`--no-ask-user` are gone). Default model `auto` (Copilot picks) — pinning a name not in the user's Copilot model set caused 'model from --model flag is not available'. `--effort`/`--reasoning-effort` choices: none/low/medium/high. Appended only when a tier is set. No non-interactive auth-status -> doctor presence-only.

agy.notes: `-p` non-interactive REQUIRES --dangerously-skip-permissions (agy's equivalent of copilot's --allow-all-tools) or it blocks on a tool-permission prompt and returns empty ('没有返回译文'). If stdout is still empty afterwards (gemini-cli #27466) -> fall back to gemini. First-call 'not logged into Antigravity' is a transient keyring cold-start read race (~1s), NOT a logout: success markers appear ~300ms later in the SAME log -> success-wins classification + bounded retry (report the final state) + local cred-file check. Do not conflate with the empty-stdout -p bug.

google-v2.notes: v2 Basic is the only edition that accepts API keys; format defaults to html so always send text; omit empty source -> auto-detect.

doubao.notes: MUST be /responses (not /chat/completions); language goes in translation_options; ~4K in/3K out chars; per-character billing; confidence medium (official docs JS-rendered).

opencode.auth: opencode auth login (creds in ~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json) or provider env keys
opencode.notes: sst/opencode TS edition (v1.17.7): prompt is a POSITIONAL arg to the `run` subcommand (NEVER -p; -p there = --password). --format json -> JSONL; answer = each type==text event's part.text concatenated. --variant tiers are provider-specific (server-validated) -> append only when set. --dangerously-skip-permissions avoids any permission-prompt hang. Spawn from a NEUTRAL CWD (loads AGENTS.md otherwise). Models are dynamic (`opencode models`); the catalog is a snapshot. PROBE is fail-wins: 'credentials' substring-matches '0 credentials' too, so failSignatures must be evaluated first. Verified live end-to-end at $0.

kimi.auth: Kimi Code OAuth (`kimi login`); creds at ~/.kimi-code/credentials/kimi-code.json
kimi.notes: Moonshot Kimi Code CLI (kimi.exe v0.17.1; NOT legacy 'kimi-cli'). `-p` runs ONE prompt non-interactively, self-contained: NO skip-permissions flag, and -p is mutually exclusive with --auto/--yolo/--plan (adding any -> exit 1). One model (kimi-code/kimi-for-coding; thinking always-on; no per-call effort -> effortTiers []). Default --output-format stream-json (NDJSON); answer = concat type==text parts. The stream-json field shape is verified-by-help, not by a real call -> best-effort; the owner's first real translate locks it. Spawn from a neutral CWD.

mimo.auth: Xiaomi MiMo API key in ~/.local/share/mimocode/auth.json (`mimo providers login`)
mimo.notes: OpenCode-derived 'mimocode' (v0.1.1). NON-INTERACTIVE = `mimo run <message>` (positional; bare `mimo` launches a TUI and HANGS). --dangerously-skip-permissions REQUIRED (--never-ask does NOT cover permissions). --variant carries reasoning effort (help exemplifies high/max/minimal; tiers are examples). Text mode leaks a `> build · <model>` chrome header line that stdout-clean can't remove -> switched to --format json (NDJSON step_start/text/step_finish; answer = each type==text event's part.text, same shape as opencode). Structure verified live; offline capture can't validate the UTF-8 (PowerShell re-encodes pipe text and mimo SUPPRESSES stdout to a non-TTY FILE — agy-#76 family — but emits to a PIPE, which is what ProcessRunner uses), so the owner's in-app test locks the final bytes. Neutral CWD.

openai-http.notes: Generic OpenAI /chat/completions long tail (DeepSeek/MiMo/Moonshot/any base_url+key). endpoint+model+apiKey ALL come from config.json (empty manifest endpoint -> ManifestHttpBackend prefers Cfg.Endpoint). {prompt} = PromptBuilder.Build(translation rules, source text) — the SAME built prompt the CLI backends send, distinct from PromptBuilder's own {content} placeholder. Custom-provider config ids with no manifest entry resolve THIS def via BackendConfig.protocol=openai. NEVER use for Kimi Code: api.kimi.com/coding returns access_terminated to OpenAI-format clients — use anthropic-http.

anthropic-http.auth: static API key (x-api-key, Bearer also accepted by Kimi); BYOK
anthropic-http.notes: Generic Anthropic /messages. REQUIRED for Kimi Code (https://api.kimi.com/coding/v1/messages) which blocks OpenAI /chat/completions (access_terminated, coding-agent allow-list). Also usable for any Claude-compatible base_url+key. endpoint+model+apiKey from config; {prompt} = PromptBuilder output. responsePath content[type=text].text uses the same arr[key=value] filter doubao uses. Custom ids resolve via BackendConfig.protocol=anthropic. Do NOT forge a coding-agent User-Agent (ToS); if Kimi 403s, fall back to the kimi CLI.
